key_insight: A multi-scale ML framework can jointly model chemical, spin, and structural disorder in alloys to predict thermodynamics and phase transitions.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical predictive tool for designing complex magnetic alloys and other disordered materials with target thermal and structural properties.
method: Combine graph neural networks and machine-learning interatomic potentials
Use Monte Carlo for configurational/spin sampling and molecular dynamics for structural evolution
Apply the framework to bcc Fe-Co with interstitial C as a multi-disorder test case
evidence_signal: The framework predicted the Fe-Co order-disorder transition at 1,000 K and melting at 1,690 K, closely matching experimental values of 1,006 K and about 1,700 K.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on the quality and transferability of the trained models and the sampled disorder states for each alloy system.
paper_title: A Multi-Scale Machine Learning Framework for Coupled Chemical, Spin, and Structural Disorder in Alloys
